title: HED 451 - Nutrition for Healthy Communities
description: Examine nutritional recommendations, challenges, and eating patterns throughout the lifespan of individuals and communities, with an emphasis on the role of nutrition in chronic disease prevention and management.

title: NRSG 348 - Transition to Professional Practice
description: Examine socialization and development of professional nursing roles. Explore theoretical models and frameworks. Develop critical thinking skills to address professional practice issues from a local to global perspective.

title: NRSG 350 - Pathophysiology Across the Lifespan
description: Develop a thorough understanding of the human body, from cellular functions to integrative body systems. Examine how disease processes impact body systems and the related therapies and treatments.

title: NRSG 372 - Health Assessment Across the Lifespan
description: Examine principles related to history taking, physical examination, diagnostic testing, health promotion, and patient education. Develop skills to conduct a comprehensive history and physical exam.

title: NRSG 390 - Informatics and Health Literacy
description: Understand information science and systems, healthcare technology, and data-driven decisions. Develop research skills to strengthen evidence-based practice for consumer-centered technology-supported care. Analyze materials for health literacy principles.

title: NRSG 460W - Leadership and Management Dynamics
description: Analyze principles related to organizational theories, socioeconomic political trends, and healthcare delivery systems. Evaluate leadership and management principles to provide comprehensive care coordination to at-risk and marginalized populations.

title: NRSG 470 - Community/Public Health Nursing
description: Examine nursing, public health, and health promotion theories. Discuss the impact of past and present policies on community health. Analyze the social determinants of health equity with a focus on at-risk and marginalized communities.

title: NRSG 471 - Community/Public Health Nursing Practicum
description: Apply nursing and public health interventions, with a focus on prevention and health promotion, to individuals, families, and communities. Evaluate systems of care for at-risk and marginalized communities.

title: NRSG 490 - Evidence-Based Practice and Research
description: Gain skills to find, understand, analyze, and critique research applicable to nursing practice. Develop professional writing and presentation skills to support evidence-based practice and advocate for change.
